concurrent.futures/ThreadPoolExecutor.name: ThreadPoolExecutor
concurrent.futures/ThreadPoolExecutor.description: An Executor subclass that uses a pool of at most max_workers threads to execute calls asynchronously. All threads enqueued to ThreadPoolExecutor will be joined before the interpreter can exit. Note that the exit handler which does this is executed before any exit handlers added using atexit. This means exceptions in the main thread must be caught and handled in order to signal threads to exit gracefully. For this reason, it is recommended that ThreadPoolExecutor not be used for long-running t...

concurrent.futures/TimeoutError.name: TimeoutError
concurrent.futures/TimeoutError.description: A deprecated alias of TimeoutError, raised when a future operation exceeds the given timeout. Changed in version 3.11: This class was made an alias of TimeoutError.

info.excerpt: A deprecated alias of TimeoutError, raised when a future operation exceeds the given timeout. Changed in version 3.11: This class was made an alias of TimeoutError.

concurrent.futures/ProcessPoolExecutor/t.name: terminate_workers
concurrent.futures/ProcessPoolExecutor/t.description: Attempt to terminate all living worker processes immediately by calling Process.terminate on each of them. Internally, it will also call Executor.shutdown() to ensure that all other resources associated with the executor are freed. After calling this method the caller should no longer submit tasks to the executor. Added in version 3.14.
